prevalence.trend: decreasing due to H. pylori eradication and PPI use

vulnerablePopulations: Immigrants from high H. pylori endemic regions
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ESGE guidelines, endoscopy with rapid urease test or histology

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: H. pylori test-and-treat strategy in some countries

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: ESGE/Maastricht VI criteria, endoscopy gold standard
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: PPIs + Maastricht VI eradication therapy (bismuth-based quadruple therapy), surveillance for complications
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Declining H. pylori (<20%) but rising NSAID-related ulcers, antibiotic resistance
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Maastricht VI guidelines, high endoscopy access, comprehensive H. pylori eradication programs
